title: [ipv6_addr.c:88] lichen_sha256 output not validated against buffer size
description:
Completeness: Buffer bounds checking category.

In lichen_pubkey_to_iid() at line 88, lichen_sha256() writes to hash[TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE] (32 bytes), then line 105 copies 8 bytes to iid.

The code is correct, but relies on implicit coupling:
1. hash is declared as uint8_t hash[TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE]
2. TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE is validated to be 32 at lichen_util.c:12
3. memcpy copies 8 bytes which is < 32

However, there is no BUILD_ASSERT in ipv6_addr.c verifying TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE >= 8. If a future refactor changed the hash algorithm to something smaller, the memcpy would overflow.

Fix: Add BUILD_ASSERT(TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE >= 8) in ipv6_addr.c near line 75.
